children.text: The term “antitrust evidence” means information, testimony, statements, documents, or other things that are obtained in anticipation of, or during the course of, an investigation or proceeding under any of the Federal antitrust laws or any of the foreign antitrust laws.

children.label: A
children.text: An assurance that the foreign antitrust authority will provide to the Attorney General and the Commission assistance that is comparable in scope to the assistance the Attorney General and the Commission provide under such agreement or such memorandum.

children.label: B
children.text: An assurance that the foreign antitrust authority is subject to laws and procedures that are adequate to maintain securely the confidentiality of antitrust evidence that may be received under section 6201, 6202, or 6203 of this title and will give protection to antitrust evidence received under such section that is not less than the protection provided under the laws of the United States to such antitrust evidence.

children.label: C
children.text: Citations to and brief descriptions of the laws of the United States, and the laws of the foreign state or regional economic integration organization represented by the foreign antitrust authority, that protect the confidentiality of antitrust evidence that may be provided under such agreement or such memorandum. Such citations and such descriptions shall include the enforcement mechanisms and penalties applicable under such laws and, with respect to a regional economic integration organization, the applicability of such laws, enforcement mechanisms, and penalties to the foreign states composing such organization.

children.label: D
children.text: Citations to the Federal antitrust laws, and the foreign antitrust laws, with respect to which such agreement or such memorandum applies.

children.label: i
children.text: for the purpose of administering or enforcing the foreign antitrust laws involved, or

children.label: I
children.text: determining that such antitrust evidence is not otherwise readily available with respect to such objective,

children.label: II
children.text: making the determinations described in paragraphs (2) and (3) of , with respect to such disclosure or use, andsection 6207(a) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: making the determinations applicable to a foreign antitrust authority under (other than the determination regarding the assurance described in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph), with respect to each additional governmental entity, if any, to be provided such antitrust evidence in the course of such disclosure or use, after having received adequate written assurances applicable to each such governmental entity.section 6207(a)(1) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: An assurance that antitrust evidence received under section 6201, 6202, or 6203 of this title from the Attorney General or the Commission, and all copies of such evidence, in the possession or control of the foreign antitrust authority will be returned to the Attorney General or the Commission, respectively, at the conclusion of the foreign investigation or proceeding with respect to which such evidence was so received.

children.label: i
children.text: the confidentiality required under such agreement or such memorandum is violated with respect to antitrust evidence, and

children.label: ii
children.text: adequate action is not taken both to minimize any harm resulting from the violation and to ensure that the confidentiality required under such agreement or such memorandum is not violated again.

children.label: i
children.text: by the foreign antitrust authority promptly to the Attorney General or the Commission with respect to antitrust evidence provided by the Attorney General or the Commission, respectively, and

children.label: ii
children.text: by the Attorney General or the Commission to the person (if any) that provided such evidence to the Attorney General or the Commission.
The term “Attorney General” means the Attorney General of the United States.
The term “Commission” means the Federal Trade Commission.

The term “Federal antitrust laws” has the meaning given the term “antitrust laws” in subsection (a) of but also includes to the extent that such section 45 applies to unfair methods of competition.section 12 of this titlesection 45 of this title

The term “foreign antitrust authority” means a governmental entity of a foreign state or of a regional economic integration organization that is vested by such state or such organization with authority to enforce the foreign antitrust laws of such state or such organization.

The term “foreign antitrust laws” means the laws of a foreign state, or of a regional economic integration organization, that are substantially similar to any of the Federal antitrust laws and that prohibit conduct similar to conduct prohibited under the Federal antitrust laws.
The term “person” has the meaning given such term in subsection (a) of .section 12 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: administering or enforcing the foreign antitrust laws of such organization, and

children.label: B
children.text: prohibiting and regulating disclosure of information that is obtained by such organization in the course of administering or enforcing such laws.
